title: ACT Manifest — single mounts entry (runtime + hybrid)
description: Extends the basic mount shape pinned by PRD-107-R5 with the runtime-specific constraints PRD-106 enforces (no recursion; absolute or origin-relative `manifest_url`; longest-prefix matching). Overlapping prefixes across the array are a manifest validation error per PRD-106-R20; that constraint is enforced at the array level and not expressible inside a single-entry schema.

prefix.description: Origin-relative URL path prefix. Matched longest-first by consumers (PRD-106-R19).

delivery.description: Per PRD-107-R5, each mount carries its own delivery profile.

manifest_url.description: Absolute or origin-relative URL of the mount's manifest. Cross-origin permitted (PRD-106-R22); origin trust is consumer-side per PRD-109.

level.description: Per PRD-107-R5, optional; inherits the parent manifest's level when omitted.

$comment: `mounts: false` enforces the no-recursion rule from PRD-106-R18 + gap C5 — a mount manifest's *entry* MUST NOT itself declare further `mounts`. The mount's referenced manifest body must also not contain a `mounts` array; that is a separate cross-document check enforced by PRD-600.
